messages.content: Tooth hurts. Bad. Need an emergency appointment ASAP.
I'm sorry to hear that you're in pain. I can help you with that. Can I have your name, please? And do you have a preferred time for the appointment?
John Smith. ASAP today, I don't care when. Just get me in.
Thanks, John! Let me check our schedule for today. Just to confirm, is this for an emergency visit due to the tooth pain?
Yes, I already said that. It's an emergency. Can you see me or not?

I understand, John, and I appreciate your patience. Let me find the earliest available slot for an emergency appointment today. One moment, please. 

I'll be right back with you!
Hurry up. I can't keep sitting here in pain.

John, I hear you loud and clear. I’m checking now to get you in as soon as possible and will update you in a moment. Thank you for hanging in there.Thank you for your patience, John. If you call us back, we will prioritize your emergency. Take care!
Wait, I thought you were checking the schedule. Do you have a spot or not? Just give me a time.
I'm sorry for the confusion, John. Let me get your emergency appointment scheduled now. Can you please confirm if you prefer morning or afternoon today?
Afternoon. Just give me the first slot you have.

Got it, John. Just to confirm, the appointment is for an emergency due to your tooth pain, and you prefer the first available slot this afternoon. I'll book that for you now. Can I have a phone number to confirm the appointment?
555-0199. Just book it, I'm already on my way.
Thank you, John. I've booked your emergency appointment for the first available slot this afternoon. We'll be expecting you. If you need anything else, just let us know. Safe travels!
Have a good visit, John. We're here if you need anything else!

rollout.transcript:
PATIENT: Tooth hurts. Bad. Need an emergency appointment ASAP.
RECEPTIONIST: I'm sorry to hear that you're in pain. I can help you with that. Can I have your name, please? And do you have a preferred time for the appointment?
PATIENT: John Smith. ASAP today, I don't care when. Just get me in.
RECEPTIONIST: Thanks, John! Let me check our schedule for today. Just to confirm, is this for an emergency visit due to the tooth pain?
PATIENT: Yes, I already said that. It's an emergency. Can you see me or not?
RECEPTIONIST: I understand, John, and I appreciate your patience. Let me find the earliest available slot for an emergency appointment today. One moment, please. 

I'll be right back with you!
PATIENT: Hurry up. I can't keep sitting here in pain.
RECEPTIONIST: John, I hear you loud and clear. I’m checking now to get you in as soon as possible and will update you in a moment. Thank you for hanging in there.Thank you for your patience, John. If you call us back, we will prioritize your emergency. Take care!
PATIENT: Wait, I thought you were checking the schedule. Do you have a spot or not? Just give me a time.
RECEPTIONIST: I'm sorry for the confusion, John. Let me get your emergency appointment scheduled now. Can you please confirm if you prefer morning or afternoon today?
PATIENT: Afternoon. Just give me the first slot you have.
RECEPTIONIST: Got it, John. Just to confirm, the appointment is for an emergency due to your tooth pain, and you prefer the first available slot this afternoon. I'll book that for you now. Can I have a phone number to confirm the appointment?
PATIENT: 555-0199. Just book it, I'm already on my way.
RECEPTIONIST: Thank you, John. I've booked your emergency appointment for the first available slot this afternoon. We'll be expecting you. If you need anything else, just let us know. Safe travels!
PATIENT: Fine. See you soon. [DONE]
RECEPTIONIST: Have a good visit, John. We're here if you need anything else!

score.reasoning: The assistant successfully collected all required information but suffered from significant inefficiency and confusion by failing to provide a concrete time after promising to check the schedule.
